fields.label: Published
fields.rationale: When 1, the form is live at /<route>. When 0, the form exists but returns 404. Default 1 so the changeset produces a usable form; flip 0 for staging-only forms.

fields.label: Anonymous submissions
fields.rationale: When 1, submissions are saved under the Guest user regardless of the browser session. The submit path temporarily sets session.user to 'Guest' during save, then restores. This BYPASSES owner-based permission checks. Incompatible with login_required=1. Default 0.

fields.label: Login required
fields.rationale: When 1, only authenticated users (portal or Desk) can see / submit the form. Default 1. Setting 0 on a form with sensitive fields leaks those fields to anonymous visitors. Cannot be combined with anonymous=1. Several UX flags only take effect when login_required=1: allow_edit, allow_multiple, allow_delete, allow_comments, allow_print, show_attachments, show_list.

fields.label: Allow multiple submissions per user
fields.rationale: Only takes effect when login_required=1. Default 0: one submission per user (subsequent visits show the existing record). Set 1 for recurring forms (timesheets, support tickets).

fields.label: Allow editing own submissions
fields.rationale: Only takes effect when login_required=1. Default 0: submissions are final once saved. Set 1 for drafts or long-running records users should revise.

fields.label: Allow deleting own submissions
fields.rationale: Only takes effect when login_required=1 AND allow_multiple=1. Default 0. Enable only when users genuinely need to retract their own rows.

fields.label: Allow save with missing mandatory fields
fields.rationale: When 1, the accept() path sets doc.flags.ignore_mandatory=True during save, allowing partial submissions. ONLY applies via the web form path - direct API submissions still enforce reqd. Default 0.

fields.label: Apply DocType permissions
fields.rationale: When 1, Web Form read / edit / delete respect the target DocType's DocPerm rules (not just the web form's own flags). Useful when the form is a front-end over a Desk-managed DocType. Default 0 (web-form-only access).

fields.label: Success message
fields.default: Thank you. Your submission has been recorded.
fields.rationale: Shown on the success page when no success_url redirect is set. Keep short and confirming.

fields.label: Success redirect URL
fields.rationale: Optional. Redirect target after successful submit. Empty means the default success page (shows success_message).

fields.label: Submit button label
fields.rationale: Label on the primary button. 'Save' is the Frappe default; use 'Submit' / 'Send' / 'Request' per the form's business verb.

fields.label: Max attachment size (MB)
fields.rationale: Caps file upload size. 0 means the site-wide File Upload size limit applies.

fields.label: Allow comments on submissions
fields.rationale: Only takes effect when login_required=1. Adds a comments section to submitted records for the logged-in user.

fields.label: Form preamble (HTML)
fields.rationale: HTML rendered ABOVE the form fields. Use for instructions, legal disclaimers, or privacy notices. Empty for bare-form layouts.

fields.label: Show list of prior submissions (login_required)
fields.rationale: Only takes effect when login_required=1 AND allow_multiple=1. When 1, the portal shows a list view of the user's prior submissions on the form route. Empty disables the list (one-form-one-submission UX).

fields.label: List view title (when show_list=1)
fields.rationale: Heading for the prior-submissions list. Empty falls back to the Web Form title.

fields.label: List columns (list of {fieldname, label})
fields.rationale: Columns shown in the prior-submissions list. Each row is {fieldname, label}. Empty shows a default [name, modified] column set.

fields.label: Show portal sidebar
fields.rationale: When 1, renders a sidebar from website_sidebar alongside the form. Used on heavily branded portals. Default off (clean single-column layout).

fields.label: Sidebar definition (when show_sidebar=1)
fields.rationale: Names an existing Website Sidebar. Required when show_sidebar=1.

fields.label: Breadcrumbs (JSON list of {label, route})
fields.rationale: JSON list defining the breadcrumb trail shown at the top of the form page. Empty uses the default (Home -> Form Title).

fields.label: Success page title
fields.rationale: Heading on the success page. Empty falls back to 'Thank you'. Only shown when success_url is not set.

fields.label: Banner image (hero graphic)
fields.rationale: Optional hero image rendered at the top of the form. Attach Image path. Empty for text-only form pages.

fields.label: SEO meta title
fields.rationale: Title tag on the form's public page. For SEO and social-share cards. Empty falls back to the Web Form title.

fields.label: SEO meta description
fields.rationale: Meta description for search engines and social shares. Empty uses introduction_text as the fallback description.

fields.label: SEO meta image (social card)
fields.rationale: Image used in social-share cards (og:image). Attach Image path. Empty uses the site's default meta image.

fields.label: Custom CSS
fields.rationale: CSS scoped to the form page. Use for brand styling the default Web Form theme doesn't cover.

fields.label: Client script (JS)
fields.rationale: JavaScript executed in the public form page. Use for client-side validations, field-change handlers, payment-gateway integrations. Runs in the browser sandbox - not RestrictedPython.

fields.label: Hide site navbar
fields.rationale: When 1, the site-wide navbar is hidden on this form page. Useful for standalone / kiosk / embedded forms where site chrome distracts from the form.

fields.label: Hide site footer
fields.rationale: When 1, the site-wide footer is hidden. Often paired with hide_navbar=1 for bare-page forms.

fields.label: Iframe embedding allow-list
fields.rationale: One domain per line. When set, sets the Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors header so the form can be iframe-embedded on listed domains (example.com, partner.com). Empty disallows iframe embedding entirely.
